tools.title: Berlin Room Heat Check
tools.lead: Most Berlin rooms have no air conditioning. Tell this tool your room type, floor, which way the windows face and the forecast, and it shows your room's summer heat risk plus the two or three moves that matter most for it.

tools.title: Jewish Museum Visit Sequence

tools.title: Markthalle Neun Week Planner
tools.lead: Markthalle Neun is a food festival on Thursday evening, a full market on Saturday, and a locked door on Sunday. Pick a day and see if the hall is open, what market or street food is on, the hours, and whether it is worth the trip.

tools.title: Berlin Money Exchange Reality Check
tools.lead: Slide to any amount and see what euro cash really costs you in Berlin four ways: a bank ATM, a city exchange office, the airport desk, or a Euronet machine with conversion. Same cash, very different price.
aliases: where to exchange money in Berlin

tools.title: Berlin Street Art: Worth the Trip?
tools.lead: See Berlin's best street art plotted by how far you have to go against how much there is to see, then tap any spot for the area, the nearest station and my honest verdict on whether it is worth the journey.
aliases: where to see street art in Berlin

tools.title: Olympiastadion Visit Planner
tools.lead: Set your Berlin day and see whether the Olympiastadion is open to walk straight in, closed for a Hertha home game or big event, or best seen at the event itself, with tickets, tour timing and my honest verdict for each way to visit.

tools.title: Berlin Viewpoint Finder
tools.lead: Compare Berlin's five public high viewpoints by real height, 2026 price and how you get up, then pick what matters most: cheapest, highest, no booking, open air or best at sunset.

tools.title: Tiergarten Loop Planner
tools.lead: Pick your Tiergarten entry station and how much time you have, and this tool plots a real walking loop through the park's landmarks in the right order, with a suggested exit station.

tools.title: Inside Teufelsberg
tools.lead: Tap through the four layers of Berlin's strangest hill: the buried Nazi college at the base, 26 million cubic metres of wartime rubble, the forest that grew over it, and the Cold War spy station on top.

tools.title: Berlin Name Pronouncer
tools.lead: Tap any Berlin station or district name to see a simple English-style respelling, the stressed syllable, the sounds that trip tourists up, and hear it spoken in German.

tools.title: Berlin Layover Planner
tools.lead: Work out how much real city time a Berlin layover gives you after the airport buffers and train rides, then see what is sensible to do, from staying airside to walking the historic centre.

tools.title: Self-Guided Prenzlauer Berg Walk
tools.lead: A self-guided half-day loop through Prenzlauer Berg in walking order: Eberswalder Strasse and Konnopke's, the Kulturbrauerei, Kollwitzplatz market, the Wasserturm, Kastanienallee and Mauerpark.

tools.title: Berlin Phone Ticket Picker
tools.lead: Tell it your plan for the day and it shows the exact Berlin transport ticket to buy in the app, the 2026 price, the fare zone, the German ticket name, and whether you need to validate it.

tools.title: Berlin Train Announcement Decoder
tools.lead: Ride one Berlin train and decode the German announcements you actually hear, from Zurückbleiben bitte to Ausstieg rechts and Ersatzverkehr, with the plain-English meaning and the one move each one asks you to make.

tools.title: Berlin Ringbahn Explorer
tools.lead: An interactive map of Berlin's Ringbahn, the S41 and S42 circle line locals call the Hundekopf. Tap any of the 27 stations to see which neighbourhood it opens up, or follow the ring clockwise (S41) and anticlockwise (S42).

tools.title: Berlin Ticket Machine Simulator
tools.lead: Practise buying a BVG ticket before you stand at a real machine in Berlin: step through the touchscreen, pick the right fare, and get a two-question nudge toward the ticket that fits your trip.

tools.title: Berlin Two-Day Route Map
tools.lead: Switch between two realistic first-visit routes, tap every stop in walking order, and see the M10 handover from the Berlin Wall Memorial to the East Side Gallery.

tools.title: German Number Decoder
tools.lead: Build any German number from 0 to 100 and hear it spoken in a real German voice. See the units-before-tens flip that makes 21 einundzwanzig (one-and-twenty), and decode the Berlin prices, platforms and quantities you actually meet.
aliases: how to say numbers in german

tools.title: World's Tallest Dinosaur Scale
tools.lead: The Giraffatitan skeleton in Berlin's Natural History Museum stands 13.27 metres tall, the tallest mounted dinosaur on Earth. Set your own height and see exactly what you are walking up to.

tools.title: Berlin Döner Order Builder
tools.lead: Build your exact döner order and get the German line to say at the counter: pick the form (Döner, Dürüm, Teller, Box), the filling, salad, sauces and extras, and see the phrase, rough pronunciation, what you get and the 2026 price.

tools.title: The Stasi, by the Numbers
tools.lead: See how deep East Germany's secret police reached: pick any group size and watch the informer and Stasi-file ratios appear.

tools.title: Treptower Park Memorial Walk
tools.lead: Walk the Soviet War Memorial in the order it was built: from the grieving mother and red granite flags to the soldier on the hill, with the distance, timing and what to notice at each stop.

tools.title: Friedrichshain Kiez Loop
tools.lead: Build a practical Friedrichshain day around Boxhagener Platz, RAW-Gelände, Karl-Marx-Allee, the East Side Gallery and Oberbaumbrücke: choose the time of day and see which stops are worth it then.

tools.title: Which Part of Neukölln?
tools.lead: Neukölln is not one place. Tell the tool what you came for, or tap a corner, and it tells you honestly whether Körnerpark, Rixdorf, the Reuterkiez canal, Karl-Marx-Straße, Sonnenallee or Hermannplatz is worth your limited time, with the nearest station and one thing to do.

tools.title: Berlin One-Day Time Planner
tools.lead: One day in Berlin is not enough to see everything, and this tool is honest about it. Set the hours you actually have and the kind of day you want, and it builds a realistic, clock-timed plan on the central historic spine from Alexanderplatz to the Brandenburg Gate, then tells you straight what will not fit.
aliases: what to do in berlin in a day

tools.title: Berlin Rooftop Sunset Planner
tools.lead: Berlin's best rooftop bars are open-air, so the evening lives and dies with the sunset. Enter a date and this planner works out tonight's Berlin sunset, tells you when to arrive, and matches five real rooftops (Klunkerkranich, Monkey Bar, House of Weekend, Deck 5, Solar) to the evening you want.

tools.title: Mehringplatz Time-Layer Viewer
tools.lead: Compare four documented views of Mehringplatz from 1894 to 2024. Use the Peace Column, the north-south axis and the changing scale of the square to read what survived, disappeared and returned in another form.

tools.title: Berlin Sightseeing Bus Value Calculator
tools.lead: Thinking about a hop-on hop-off pass in Berlin? Set your days, group and walking style, and this calculator lays the sightseeing-bus price next to a BVG day ticket with Bus 100/200 and a tip-based walking tour, then gives a clear verdict with live 2026 numbers.
aliases: is the berlin bus tour worth it

tools.title: Berlin Trip Length Advisor
tools.lead: How many days do you need in Berlin? how many days do you need in berlin

tools.title: Berlin Walking Distance Checker
tools.lead: Is Berlin walkable? Pick where you are starting and see how many minutes it really takes to walk to every other main sight, with an honest verdict on when to stroll and when to take the U-Bahn.
aliases: how far apart are berlin sights

tools.title: Berlin U-Bahn Etiquette Cheat Sheet
tools.lead: Current Berlin transport rules and useful local habits, separated by situation: doors, escalators, seats, food, bikes and tickets.
aliases: how to behave on berlin u-bahn

tools.title: Marathon Day Berlin
tools.lead: Play Berlin Marathon Sunday hour by hour. Compare the confirmed 2026 starts with the 2025 closure pattern, then check live transport before you cross the city.
tags: how to cross the marathon course

tools.title: Berlin Ghost Stations Map
tools.lead: The 15 Berlin stations that were sealed when the Wall went up. Flip the same tunnel map between 1961 and today, then tap a station to see what happened there and what is there now.

tools.title: Ku'damm Axis Walk
tools.lead: Choose a stop on the 1.3 to 1.4 km visitor axis from Wittenbergplatz to Uhlandstraße and see what is worth your time before you keep walking west.

tools.title: Berlin Beer Season Calendar
tools.lead: Enter your arrival and departure dates to see which confirmed 2026 Berlin beer and Oktoberfest events overlap your stay, then compare them with Munich.

tools.title: Berlin Underground Tour Board
tools.lead: The Berlin underground bunker tours do not all start at Gesundbrunnen. Set the youngest person in your group and the month you are here, and see which tours you can actually book and which street door each one uses.

tools.title: Spreewald Reach Map
tools.lead: Punt tour or kayak, two hours or a full day: every way onto the water at Lübbenau covers a different slice of the Spreewald. Pick one and the map shows what you actually reach, with 2026 prices.

tools.title: Schöneberg Plaque Check
tools.lead: Schöneberg keeps its history on small plaques screwed to buildings people still live in. Set the bar you care about, from a plaque to a proper exhibition, pick your day, and see which addresses are worth the U-Bahn ride.

tools.title: Dresden Day Clock
tools.lead: A Dresden day trip from Berlin is an hours problem, not a distance problem. Pick your train, your departure and your return, and see how much of Dresden is actually left once the journey and the walk in from the station are paid for.
